Naming mismatch: platform value is 'mcp_server' but file is 'mcp-server.md'. Treated as bug below.

framework_bugs.summary: Platform module filename mismatch: mcp_server vs mcp-server
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --platform mcp_server ... ; ls docs/platform-modules/ in generated project (empty); ls $FRAMEWORK/docs/platform-modules/ shows mcp-server.md (hyphen).
framework_bugs.expected: init copies the platform module into the project; verify-install does NOT warn 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'.
framework_bugs.actual: Generated project's docs/platform-modules/ is empty. verify-install reports MANUAL: 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing -> Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md'. The actual framework file is docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md. The platform identifier (mcp_server with underscore) used in init flags and phase-state.json does not match the on-disk filename (mcp-server.md with hyphen). Either init.sh's copy step uses the wrong identifier, or the file should be renamed.

documentation_gaps.gap: release.yml not created when --git-host other is selected; init prints '[INFO] Host other — no release template laid down' but Section A test 'release_workflow_created' assumes it should always be created. Either docs the host=other behavior in Section A test rubric, or extend init.sh to generate a generic release.yml stub for host=other.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh:2304 (case 'other' -> early return) and RUNBOOK.md Section A 'release_workflow_created'

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: upgrade-project.sh --to-production from sponsored_poc baseline exits RC=0; output ends with '[OK] Upgrade complete.' U-E (poc_mode lookup from phase-state.json) confirmed working — script printed 'Current POC mode: sponsored poc'.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .current_phase preserved (0 -> 0); .project preserved (uat-agent-82). Diff shows only intentional changes: track light->standard, poc_mode field removed, last_upgrade added.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: diff /tmp/uat-82-pre-process.json .claude/process-state.json -> empty. .build_loop history and feature records intact. build-progress.json unchanged: features_completed = ['uat-feat-1'].

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output names: 'Review CLAUDE.md for any remaining POC references' and 'Review PROJECT_INTAKE.md Section 8 governance fields'; also names new track requirements ('Check docs/reference/builders-guide.md for standard track requirements'). Light guidance but present.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Restored pre-upgrade phase-state, wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema, ran upgrade-project.sh --to-production: exits RC=1 with '[FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision.' and remediation hints. PR #17 U-F confirmed.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header changelog mentions BL-006 (2026-04-24), BL-015 (2026-04-25), BL-016 (2026-04-25).

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep -E '__PROJECT_NAME__|__PLATFORM__|__LANGUAGE__|__TRACK__' /tmp/uat-82-upg-clean.log returned nothing. U-I not observed in this config (still open per runbook, but did not surface).

tests.name: deployment_field_write_on_upgrade
tests.details: U-J confirmation: after --to-production, .deployment in phase-state.json remains 'organizational' rather than being updated to 'production'. Also, .poc_mode is removed entirely (set to null then dropped) instead of being set to 'production'. Subsequent re-runs of upgrade-project.sh --to-production now fail with '[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode.' (RC=0 though). Confirmation of prior-sweep U-J — still open.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: deployment/poc_mode fields not properly written on --to-production
framework_bugs.reproduction: Build sponsored_poc baseline, complete Phase 1, record one feature, run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production, then jq '.deployment, .poc_mode' .claude/phase-state.json.
framework_bugs.expected: After --to-production, phase-state.json should record the production deployment/mode (e.g., poc_mode='production' or removed-and-deployment-becomes-'production'). Re-running upgrade should be idempotent or report 'already production'.
framework_bugs.actual: .deployment unchanged ('organizational'). .poc_mode initially set to null after first upgrade, then field dropped entirely after second invocation. On re-run, script prints '[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode. Use --track and/or --deployment for non-POC upgrades.' but exits RC=0 — exit code does not match FAIL semantics. Confirms U-J is still open.

framework_bugs.reproduction: Run upgrade-project.sh --to-production twice on the same project (first succeeds, second hits the 'not in POC mode' branch).
framework_bugs.expected: When a [FAIL] line is printed, the script should exit non-zero so callers/CI can detect the failure.
framework_bugs.actual: Second invocation prints '[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode. Use --track and/or --deployment for non-POC upgrades.' but exits 0. Inconsistent with set -euo pipefail and Critical-severity guidance for happy-path scripts.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep matches were all framework boilerplate (e.g., 'GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}' in ci.yml, the words 'secret/token/key' inside reference docs). No literal credential values. Safe.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ (the results dir we write to). git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches captured SHA).

summary: Section A init mostly clean (1 Medium bug — platform-module filename mismatch); Section F upgrade flow works (RC=0, sentinel respected, U-E/U-F/PR #17 fixes verified) but confirms U-J still open and surfaces a Low bug where upgrade-project.sh prints [FAIL] yet exits 0; Section G post-flight clean.
